etymology.description: semantic 木(木) (mù, "tree") + phonetic 耶(耶) (yē) -> coconut
etymology.interpretation: The semantic component 木(木) (mù, "tree") indicates that 椰(yē, "coconut") is related to trees, specifically the coconut tree, while the phonetic component 耶(耶) (yē) suggests the pronunciation.

examples.chinese: 椰子的汁很甜美(椰子的汁很甜美)。
examples.english: The juice of the coconut is very sweet.

examples.chinese: 他们在沙滩上喝椰子水(他們在沙灘上喝椰子水)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen zài shātān shàng hē yēzi shuǐ.
examples.english: They are drinking coconut water on the beach.

examples.chinese: 我喜欢把椰子和水果一起搅拌(我喜歡把椰子和水果一起攪拌)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ xǐhuān bǎ yēzi hé shuǐguǒ yīqǐ jiǎobàn.
examples.english: I like to mix coconut with fruits.
